I'm implementing US-057 for the Marketify project (Expo + RN + Supabase, working dir `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify`). Story: "Application review with eligibility re-check + override dialog". Acceptance criteria:

1. Tap a pending application → review sheet with creator metrics + pitch
2. Approve button triggers a new edge function `decide-application` (write it now): re-runs eligibility, returns `{ ok: true }` or `{ ok: false, drift: string[] }` so the UI can render the override dialog
3. Override confirms with second tap → application becomes approved regardless
4. Reject button + reason field
5. mobile-mcp verification with screenshots

I need a thorough map of the EXISTING code before I implement. Please research and report (under 500 words):

A. **Lister inbox applications screen (US-056 already shipped):** path of the file, how it fetches applications via `list_my_applications_as_lister()`, what fields each row has (joined from `listing_versions`, `creator_profiles`, `users`, `social_links`), and how/where I should hook in the "tap a card → open review sheet" navigation.

B. **`_shared/eligibility.ts`:** what function names + signatures are exported, what input shape the pre-conditions take, and how to call it from a brand-new `decide-application` edge function (analogue to how `apply-to-listing` and `get-listing-detail` use it).

C. **`apply_to_listing_rpc` migration (US-041) + the apply-to-listing edge function:** exact pattern of "version-pin + FOR UPDATE re-read" — I need to do the same on the lister side: re-read `listings.current_version_id`, lock the application row, then update its status to approved/rejected. Show me the SQL skeleton and how the edge function calls it.

D. **`applications` RLS policies (look in `supabase/migrations/us_009_*` and any later patches):** what's the exact policy name + condition for a lister to UPDATE an application's status? If only `_creator_rw` and `_lister_read` exist (no lister UPDATE), I'll need to write the mutation through a SECURITY DEFINER RPC instead.

E. **BottomSheet primitive + ApplySheet (US-041 creator-side):** quickly summarize the pattern for an editable multi-section bottom sheet (header + scroll body + sticky actions row), and how to do an inline confirmation modal/dialog (prefer the centered confirmation modal pattern from US-055 `CascadeConfirmModal` for the override + reject-confirm flow per Pattern #129).

F. **`decide-application` edge function — does it already exist?** Grep `supabase/functions/` and `supabase/migrations/` for "decide_application" / "decide-application" / "approve_application" to make sure I'm not duplicating.

G. **Existing screen file naming under `app/(lister)/`:** confirm the inbox-applications path so I know where to route the review screen (or whether to keep it as a sheet inside the inbox). Also check if there's any existing "application detail" route stub.

H. Confirm the docs/tech-architecture.md §5 spec for the decide-application contract — search for "decide-application" or "decide_application" in `docs/tech-architecture.md`, quote the relevant lines (response shape, error codes, override flag) verbatim.

Keep it terse. Use file_path:line refs. Don't propose implementation — just map what exists.

1	# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document
2	
3	**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
4	**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
5	**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Design Decisions
10	
11	The table below captures every non-obvious architectural choice. Each row is a committed default that the rest of the document relies on — if you need to revisit one, expect ripple effects in §4 (schema), §5 (edge functions), and `design.md` (UX).
12	
13	### 1.1 Identity & Auth
14	
15	| Area | Decision |
16	|---|---|
17	| Metric collection | **Apify scrapers, no OAuth.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP. |
18	| Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership. |
19	| Creator + lister on same account | One role per account. Dual-role users make two accounts. |
20	| Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |
21	| Handle impersonation | No ownership verification in MVP — first registrant wins. Impersonation is a manual-report problem; accept until it becomes real abuse. |
22	
23	### 1.2 Listing Lifecycle
24	
25	| Area | Decision |
26	|---|---|
27	| States | `draft → active → (paused \| closed) → archived`. No `expired` state. |
28	| Auto-expiration | None. `end_date` column retained for v1.1; listers close listings manually. |
29	| Submission cap | `max_submissions` nullable. Listers close manually when they're done — no auto-close trigger in v1. |
30	| Application uniqueness | One active application per `(creator, listing)`. Partial unique index. |
31	| Cascade-on-edit | Editing any versioned field — price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions — bumps a version and cascade-cancels pending applications with `cancelled_listing_edit`. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |
32	
33	### 1.3 Applications & Submissions
34	
35	| Area | Decision |
36	|---|---|
37	| Withdraw | Creator can withdraw from `pending` → `withdrawn`. |
38	| Eligibility drift between apply and approve | Re-check at approve-time. If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |
39	| Videos per submission | 1..N video URLs per submission. `min_videos` post-condition (default 1). |
40	| Revision request | Binary approve/reject only. Column reserved for v1.1. |
41	| Reapplication | After `rejected` / `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. |
42	| Submission reuse | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Surfaced to the lister via `ReuseBadge` (design §4.6) but not blocked. |
43	| Disputes | Append-only `events` table; support email only in MVP. |
44	| Pending apps/submissions expiry | No auto-expire. Rows stay in-inbox until explicitly acted on. |
45	
46	### 1.4 Money
47	
48	| Area | Decision |
49	|---|---|
50	| Payments | No real money in MVP. `price` is display-only. Off-platform handshake. `payment_status` column stubbed for v2. |
51	
52	### 1.5 Notifications
53	
54	| Area | Decision |
55	|---|---|
56	| Events | Application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected; listing version changed; metrics refresh failed. |
57	| Channels | In-app inbox + Supabase Realtime. Push deferred to v1.1. |
58	| Transport | Realtime on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`. Poll fallback on app resume. |
59	
60	### 1.6 Metrics / Apify
61	
62	| Area | Decision |
63	|---|---|
64	| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
65	| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
66	| Video integrity | Validate via TikTok/IG oEmbed at submission time (`422` if 404). Nightly sweep re-validates `approved` submissions — flags but doesn't change state. |
67	| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |
68	
69	### 1.7 Security / Abuse
70	
71	| Area | Decision |
72	|---|---|
73	| Spam/illegal listings | `report_listing` button → admin table. Admin reviews via Supabase Studio. |
74	| Submission cross-posting | Flag via `ReuseBadge`, don't hard-block. |
75	
76	---
77	
78	## 2. State Machines
79	
80	Notation: `transition [actor: user|lister|system|time]`.
81	
82	### 2.1 Listing
83	
84	```
85	(init) ──create[lister]──> draft
86	draft ──publish[lister]──> active
87	active ──pause[lister]──> paused
88	paused ──resume[lister]──> active
89	active ──close[lister]──> closed
90	closed ──archive[lister]──> archived
91	paused ──archive[lister]──> archived
92	
93	Side-effect on edit mutating price/pre-cond/post-cond/samples/max_submissions while active:
94	  version++ AND cascade-cancel pending applications (see §2.3)
95	```
96	
97	Terminal: `archived`.
98	
99	### 2.2 Application

134	
135	```
136	(init, on link or refresh) ──apify_run_started[system]──> refreshing
137	refreshing ──success[system|apify_webhook]──> fresh
138	refreshing ──fail[system]──> failed
139	fresh ──age > 24h[time]──> stale
140	stale ──run_started[creator_manual | system_daily]──> refreshing
141	failed ──retry[system|creator]──> refreshing
142	```

144	Rule: eligibility filter uses `fresh` OR `stale`, never `failed`. UI warns if `stale`.
145

147	
148	## 3. Apify Integration
149	
150	**Sources:**
151	- `apify-client` NPM package — [github.com/apify/apify-client-js](https://github.com/apify/apify-client-js)
152	- TikTok Scraper actor page — [apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper](https://apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper)
153	- Instagram Scraper actor page — [apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper](https://apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper)
154	
155	### 3a. Actor Catalog (v1)
156	
157	We use **two actors only**, consolidating all metric fetches:
158	
159	| Platform | Actor Slug | REST slug (with `~`) | Purpose |
160	|---|---|---|---|
161	| TikTok | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` | `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` | Profile + last N videos in a single run |
162	| Instagram | `apify/instagram-scraper` | `apify~instagram-scraper` | Profile details **and** posts/reels — two runs per refresh (different `resultsType`) |
163	
164	**Actor endpoints** (both actors expose the same surface):
165	
166	- Sync (blocking, returns dataset items): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/run-sync-get-dataset-items?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
167	- Async (returns run, webhook-completed): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
168	
169	In practice we use the `apify-client` JS SDK (`.actor(slug).call()` for sync, `.actor(slug).start()` for async) — it wraps both endpoints.
170	
171	### 3b. TikTok — `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`
172	
173	**Input (profile scrape):**
174	```json
175	{
176	  "profiles": ["<handle>"],
177	  "resultsPerPage": 10,
178	  "profileScrapeSections": ["videos"],
179	  "profileSorting": "latest",
180	  "excludePinnedPosts": true,
181	  "shouldDownloadVideos": false,
182	  "shouldDownloadCovers": false,
183	  "shouldDownloadSubtitles": false
184	}
185	```
186	
187	Notes:
188	- `profileSorting: "latest"` returns newest videos first — required so `avg_views_last_10` is the **recent** trailing window, not all-time popular.
189	- `excludePinnedPosts: true` avoids skewing the average with a single mega-viral pinned post.
190	- Download flags default to `true` on Apify; we override to `false` to cut run cost (we never need the media bytes).
191	
192	**Output** — one row per video, with the author profile **denormalized onto every row**. Relevant fields:
193	
194	| Path | Meaning | Maps to |
195	|---|---|---|
196	| `authorMeta.name` | handle | verification vs. input |
197	| `authorMeta.fans` | follower count | `follower_count` |
198	| `authorMeta.following` | following count | (stored for profile, not eligibility) |
199	| `authorMeta.heart` | total lifetime likes across videos | (stored) |
200	| `authorMeta.video` | total post count | `video_count` |
201	| `authorMeta.verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |
202	| `playCount` | **view count for this video** | contributes to `avg_views_last_10` |
203	| `diggCount` | like count for this video | (stored) |
204	| `shareCount`, `commentCount`, `collectCount` | engagement | (stored) |
205	| `videoMeta.duration` | seconds | (stored) |
206	| `createTimeISO`, `webVideoUrl` | timestamps / permalink | (stored) |
207	
208	**Metric derivation (TikTok):**
209	- `follower_count = items[0].authorMeta.fans`
210	- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(items[i].playCount for i in 0..min(10, len(items)))`
211	- `video_count = items[0].authorMeta.video`
212	
213	**One run per refresh.**
214	
215	### 3c. Instagram — `apify/instagram-scraper`
216	
217	The Instagram actor is a **multi-mode scraper**; what it returns depends on `resultsType`. For our use case we need two modes per refresh:
218	
219	**Run 1 — profile details (follower count):**
220	```json
221	{
222	  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
223	  "resultsType": "details",
224	  "resultsLimit": 1,
225	  "addParentData": false
226	}
227	```
228	
229	Output (one row, keyed on username):
230	
231	| Field | Meaning | Maps to |
232	|---|---|---|
233	| `username` | handle | verification |
234	| `followersCount` | follower count | `follower_count` |
235	| `followsCount` | following count | (stored) |
236	| `postsCount` | total posts | `media_count` |
237	| `fullName`, `biography`, `profilePicUrl` | profile chrome | (stored) |
238	| `isBusinessAccount` | is Business/Creator | (stored; informational only — **not required**) |
239	| `verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |
240	
241	**Run 2 — recent reels (view counts):**
242	```json
243	{
244	  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
245	  "resultsType": "posts",
246	  "resultsLimit": 10,
247	  "addParentData": false
248	}
249	```
250	
251	`resultsType: "posts"` returns the profile's recent feed (posts + reels interleaved). We filter client-side to `type === "Video"` entries and use their view counts.
252	
253	Output (one row per post) — relevant fields:
254	
255	| Field | Meaning | Notes |
256	|---|---|---|
257	| `type` | `"Image"` \| `"Video"` \| `"Sidecar"` | filter to `"Video"` |
258	| `videoPlayCount` | Instagram's "plays" metric | primary signal |
259	| `videoViewCount` | view metric (sometimes null on older posts) | fallback |
260	| `likesCount`, `commentsCount` | engagement | (stored) |
261	| `videoDuration` | seconds | (stored) |
262	| `caption`, `hashtags`, `mentions`, `timestamp`, `url`, `shortCode` | post meta | (stored) |
263	
264	**Metric derivation (Instagram):**
265	- `follower_count = details[0].followersCount`
266	- `media_count = details[0].postsCount`
267	- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(p.videoPlayCount ?? p.videoViewCount for p in posts where p.type === "Video")` — skip the profile entirely if there are fewer than 1 video posts (set `avg_views_last_10 = null`, surface in UI as "Not enough video posts to compute").
268	
269	**Photo-only creators:** if `posts` returns 0 `type === "Video"` entries across 10 results, we write `instagram_avg_views_last_10 = null` and surface it in the UI as "Not enough video posts to compute." **No fallback run to `resultsType: "reels"`** — the cost doubling (extra ~$0.007/refresh × every photo-only creator × daily cron) wasn't justified in back-of-envelope math vs. just telling the creator to post more reels. Listings that require `min_avg_views_last_n` on Instagram treat `null` as ineligible (fail-closed).
270	
271	**Two runs per IG refresh.** Budget: 1 run = TikTok refresh, 2 runs = IG refresh. No conditional third run.
272

295

299	  .start(

16	-- (that runs under the UPDATE's own row lock on listings). Closes the
17	-- race described in docs/tech-architecture.md §5.5 where an applicant's
18	-- INSERT could interleave with a lister's version bump and end up pinned
19	-- to an already-stale version.
20	--
21	-- Eligibility — NOT re-evaluated inside the RPC. The edge function runs
22	-- the pre-condition evaluator in TS against the same version_id it then
23	-- passes as p_expected_version_id. If the version changes between the
24	-- edge function's read and this RPC's lock, the RPC returns
25	-- LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED and the client re-opens the detail view to
26	-- re-evaluate. This avoids duplicating the eligibility engine in
27	-- PL/pgSQL while still matching the §5.5 "re-run eligibility against the
28	-- NOW-current version_id" invariant via the version-id pin.
29	--
30	-- This invariant depends on `app_private.bump_listing_version` producing a
31	-- new `current_version_id` for every mutation that can change eligibility
32	-- (listing_conditions, body fields the trigger watches). If a future
33	-- trigger change lets an eligibility-affecting edit land without bumping
34	-- `current_version_id`, this RPC would silently commit against stale TS-
35	-- evaluated eligibility — keep the trigger and the condition-set coupled.
36	--
37	-- Auth: revoked from anon/authenticated/public. Only service_role (used
38	-- by the edge function) may execute.
39	
40	create or replace function public.apply_to_listing_rpc(
41	  p_listing_id uuid,
42	  p_creator_id uuid,
43	  p_expected_version_id uuid,
44	  p_cover_note text default null
45	) returns jsonb
46	language plpgsql
47	set search_path = ''
48	as $$
49	declare
50	  v_status public.listing_status;
51	  v_current_version_id uuid;
52	  v_application_id uuid;
53	begin
54	  select l.status, l.current_version_id
55	    into v_status, v_current_version_id
56	    from public.listings l
57	   where l.id = p_listing_id
58	     for update;
59	
60	  if not found then
61	    return jsonb_build_object('error', 'LISTING_NOT_FOUND');
62	  end if;
63	
64	  if v_status <> 'active'::public.listing_status then
65	    return jsonb_build_object('error', 'LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE');
66	  end if;
67	
68	  if v_current_version_id is null then
69	    -- Data-integrity guard: an active listing without a current_version_id
70	    -- is a persistence bug; re-raise so the edge function surfaces as 500.
71	    raise exception 'listing % has no current_version_id', p_listing_id;
72	  end if;
73	
74	  if v_current_version_id <> p_expected_version_id then
75	    return jsonb_build_object(
76	      'error', 'LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED',
77	      'current_version_id', v_current_version_id
78	    );
79	  end if;
80	
81	  -- Active-application gate mirrors the partial unique index on
82	  -- (listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status IN ('pending','approved'). An
83	  -- explicit pre-check under the row lock yields a clean 409 rather than
84	  -- requiring unique_violation diagnostics; the partial index remains
85	  -- the DB-level guarantee.
86	  if exists (
87	    select 1
88	      from public.applications a
89	     where a.listing_id = p_listing_id
90	       and a.creator_id = p_creator_id
91	       and a.status in (
92	         'pending'::public.application_status,
93	         'approved'::public.application_status
94	       )
95	  ) then
96	    return jsonb_build_object('error', 'ALREADY_APPLIED');
97	  end if;
98	
99	  insert into public.applications (
100	    listing_id, listing_version_id, creator_id, status, cover_note
101	  ) values (
102	    p_listing_id, v_current_version_id, p_creator_id,
103	    'pending'::public.application_status, p_cover_note
104	  )
105	  returning id into v_application_id;
106	
107	  return jsonb_build_object(
108	    'application_id', v_application_id,
109	    'listing_version_id', v_current_version_id
110	  );
111	end;
112	$$;
113	
114	revoke all on function public.apply_to_listing_rpc(uuid, uuid, uuid, text) from public;
115	revoke all on function public.apply_to_listing_rpc(uuid, uuid, uuid, text) from anon, authenticated;
116	grant execute on function public.apply_to_listing_rpc(uuid, uuid, uuid, text) to service_role;

1	-- US-056 — Lister Inbox: Applications tab RPC.
2	--
3	-- Contract: `public.list_my_applications_as_lister()` returns every
4	-- application (status in pending/approved/rejected) targeting a listing
5	-- owned by the caller (`listings.lister_id = public.current_user_id()`),
6	-- joined to the creator's username + tiktok/instagram handles +
7	-- denormalized follower/view metrics. One row per application.
8	--
9	-- Why SECURITY DEFINER + explicit ownership check (Codebase Pattern #118,
10	-- #117): a plain PostgREST embed on `applications` would need to reach
11	-- `users.username` (users_self_select RLS is self-only → silent null),
12	-- `social_links.handle` (social_links_lister_read RLS only shows rows
13	-- whose user has applied to the caller's listings — works for this
14	-- screen but is fragile to future tightening), and
15	-- `creator_profiles.*_follower_count` (creator_profiles_lister_read
16	-- analogous). DEFINER plus an explicit lister_id gate collapses all
17	-- four RLS checks into one authoritative ownership filter, and matches
18	-- the `list_my_applications` (US-043) / `list_my_campaigns` (US-054)
19	-- precedent shape.
20	--
21	-- Status filter: AC scopes the inbox to Pending / Approved / Rejected.
22	-- Three enum values — `withdrawn`, `cancelled_listing_edit`,
23	-- `cancelled_listing_closed` — are intentionally excluded from this
24	-- inbox because they represent creator-initiated or cascade-driven
25	-- cancellations that do not require lister review. They remain visible
26	-- on the campaign-scoped application detail screens (US-057+).
27	--
28	-- Social-link join semantics: filter by `status <> 'unlinked'` so rows
29	-- with a subsequently removed handle (history-preserved per US-012
30	-- design) are omitted. The partial unique index
31	-- `social_links_active_uniq (user_id, platform) WHERE status <> 'unlinked'`
32	-- guarantees at most one active link per (user, platform), so the
33	-- left-join cannot duplicate the applications row. When a creator has
34	-- not linked a platform at all, the handle/metrics columns come back
35	-- NULL and the UI surfaces the "no handle" state inline.
36	--
37	-- Ordering: `created_at desc` — freshest applications first. This
38	-- matches `list_my_applications` (US-043) and the inbox UX spec
39	-- (docs/design.md §3.2: "grouped by campaign" — the UI is responsible
40	-- for SectionHeader grouping; the RPC provides the data in recency
41	-- order so the intra-campaign cells remain recency-ordered).
42	--
43	-- Codebase Patterns referenced:
44	--   #117 Audit PostgREST embeds against target-table RLS
45	--   #118 SECURITY DEFINER + ownership-check list-my-rows skeleton
46	--   #119 `list_my_<things>` naming convention; argumentless; public schema
47	--   #42  `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify every user-defined ref

49	create or replace function public.list_my_applications_as_lister()
50	returns table (
51	  application_id              uuid,
52	  status                      public.application_status,
53	  created_at                  timestamptz,
54	  cover_note                  text,
55	  listing_id                  uuid,
56	  listing_title               text,
57	  creator_user_id             uuid,
58	  creator_username            text,
59	  tiktok_handle               text,
60	  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
61	  instagram_handle            text,
62	  instagram_follower_count    integer,
63	  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer
64	)
65	language sql
66	stable
67	security definer
68	set search_path = ''
69	as $$
70	  select a.id                              as application_id,
71	         a.status,
72	         a.created_at,
73	         a.cover_note,
74	         a.listing_id,
75	         l.title                           as listing_title,
76	         a.creator_id                      as creator_user_id,
77	         u.username::text                  as creator_username,
78	         tt.handle::text                   as tiktok_handle,
79	         cp.tiktok_follower_count,
80	         ig.handle::text                   as instagram_handle,
81	         cp.instagram_follower_count,
82	         cp.instagram_avg_views_last_10
83	    from public.applications a
84	    join public.listings l
85	      on l.id = a.listing_id
86	    left join public.users u
87	      on u.id = a.creator_id
88	    left join public.creator_profiles cp
89	      on cp.user_id = a.creator_id
90	    left join public.social_links tt
91	      on tt.user_id = a.creator_id
92	     and tt.platform = 'tiktok'::public.platform
93	     and tt.status   <> 'unlinked'::public.social_link_status
94	    left join public.social_links ig
95	      on ig.user_id = a.creator_id
96	     and ig.platform = 'instagram'::public.platform
97	     and ig.status   <> 'unlinked'::public.social_link_status
98	   where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
99	     and a.status in (
100	       'pending'::public.application_status,
101	       'approved'::public.application_status,
102	       'rejected'::public.application_status
103	     )
104	   order by a.created_at desc;
105	$$;

324	**Signup flow (user waiting):** Use sync `.call(input, { waitSecs: 60 })` for the *first* scrape per handle. If the actor finishes inside 60s, populate the snapshot inline; if it doesn't, the `waitSecs` limit returns a `RUNNING` run and the webhook completes it later. UI shows "Fetching your metrics..." until either returns.
325	
326	**Cron refresh / manual refresh:** Always async (`.start()` with webhook). The refresh endpoint queues work and returns `202 Accepted` immediately.
327	
328	### 3e. Metric Derivation — Summary
329	
330	| Denormalized field on `creator_profiles` | TikTok source | Instagram source |
331	|---|---|---|
332	| `tiktok_follower_count` | `authorMeta.fans` | — |
333	| `tiktok_avg_views_last_10` | `mean(playCount[:10])` | — |
334	| `tiktok_video_count` | `authorMeta.video` | — |
335	| `tiktok_is_verified` | `authorMeta.verified` | — |
336	| `instagram_follower_count` | — | `details[0].followersCount` |
337	| `instagram_avg_views_last_10` | — | `mean(videoPlayCount ?? videoViewCount)` across `type === "Video"` posts |
338	| `instagram_media_count` | — | `details[0].postsCount` |
339	| `instagram_is_verified` | — | `details[0].verified` *(reserved; column add in v1.1)* |
340	
341	Stored fully in `metric_snapshots.raw_payload` (jsonb) for forensics and future backfills — if we later need `diggCount` trends we don't need to re-scrape.
342	
343	### 3f. Cost & Rate Management
344	
345	- Every actor run costs Apify credits. Published pricing at time of writing: TikTok Scraper ≈ $0.40 / 1k videos (so ~$0.004 per 10-video run), Instagram Scraper ≈ $2.30 / 1k results. Budget conservatively at ~$0.01 per TikTok refresh and ~$0.02 per IG refresh (details + 10 posts = 11 results).
346	- **Scale math (back-of-envelope):** 10k creators × daily cron refresh ≈ $40/day TikTok-only, $200/day IG-only, $240/day fully-linked. That's ~$7.2k/month worst case. Sensitivity: cutting daily cron to every 48h halves that; capping free-tier creators to weekly refresh quarters it.
347	- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` returned before any Apify call.
348	- **Daily cron enforcement:** `cron-refresh-metrics` edge function pulls `social_links` ordered by `metrics_fetched_at ASC NULLS FIRST` and keeps a running tally of `estimated_cost_usd`; it stops dispatching new runs once `estimated_cost_usd >= APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET_USD`. Skipped links get picked up first the next day because of the `NULLS FIRST` ordering and oldest-first tie-break. Estimate is static per `scrape_mode` (`tiktok_profile`: $0.005, `ig_details`: $0.005, `ig_posts`: $0.015) — no dynamic pricing lookup needed.
349	- Set Apify run timeout (via `timeoutSecs` input to `.start()`) to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.
350	- Memory: default `memoryMbytes` is fine for both actors (1024 MB); don't override.
351	
352	### 3g. Webhook Security
353	
354	- We attach `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret: <token>` via `headersTemplate` on every webhook registration. Edge Function rejects requests without a matching secret; compare via `crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual`-equivalent (constant-time).
355	- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` has a partial unique index, so redelivery of the same `run_id` no-ops on the second write.
356	- Webhook event types registered: `ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED`, `ACTOR.RUN.FAILED`, `ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT`, `ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED`. All four resolve the snapshot (either `fresh` or `failed`).
357	
358	**Secret rotation procedure (dual-secret window):**
359	1. Edge function accepts EITHER `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (current) OR `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` (grace). Both env vars are read and compared in constant time; either match succeeds.
360	2. Set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS = <current>`, then generate a new random 32-byte token and set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET = <new>`.
361	3. New Apify runs registered after this change carry the new secret in their `headersTemplate`. In-flight runs (registered with the previous secret) continue to deliver successfully because the edge function still accepts the previous secret.
362	4. After the longest possible run duration (we set `timeoutSecs: 120` + Apify processing ≤ 10 min buffer = **15 minutes**), the janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` will have fail-closed any surviving snapshots. Unset `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` to complete rotation.
363	
364	### 3h. Rendering Submitted Videos
365	
366	oEmbed for both:
367	- TikTok: `GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>` → `html`, `thumbnail_url`.
368	- Instagram: `GET https://graph.facebook.com/v<version>/instagram_oembed?url=<reel_url>&access_token=<app_token>` → thumbnail + attribution. (Note: this needs an app-level Meta token, NOT a creator token; that's still required for oEmbed but is a one-time app setup, not per-user OAuth.)
369	- Alternative: construct TikTok embed URL `https://www.tiktok.com/embed/v2/<video_id>` directly.

373	## 4. Supabase Database Schema
374	
375	Applied from `supabase-postgres-best-practices`: RLS enabled on all public tables; FKs indexed; partial indexes for hot filters; `security_invoker` on views; `security definer` functions in private schema.
376	
377	### 4.1 Tables Overview
378	
379	| Table | Purpose |
380	|---|---|
381	| `users` | Unified account row; role-gated. |
382	| `creator_profiles` | 1:1 extension; denormalized metrics for fast filtering. |
383	| `lister_profiles` | 1:1 extension for lister fields. |
384	| `social_links` | Creator's linked handles (TikTok and/or IG). No tokens — we don't OAuth. |
385	| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link, one Apify run per row. |
386	| `listings` | Core campaign row; mutable fields. |
387	| `listing_versions` | Immutable versioned snapshots. |
388	| `listing_conditions` | Pre + post conditions, structured rows. |
389	| `sample_videos` | Sample video URLs per version. |
390	| `applications` | Creator → listing_version. |
391	| `submissions` | Deliverables on an approved application. |
392	| `submission_videos` | N video URLs per submission. |
393	| `notifications` | In-app inbox. |
394	| `push_tokens` | Expo push (v1.1). |
395	| `events` | Append-only audit log. |

429	### 4.3 Denormalization
430	
431	- `creator_profiles.tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_follower_count`, `_avg_views_last_10`, `metrics_fetched_at` — denormalized from latest `metric_snapshots` via trigger. Enables O(log n) feed filtering.
432	- `listings.current_version_id` — FK to `listing_versions`, avoids feed join.
433	- `listings.approved_submissions_count`, `active_pending_applications_count` — trigger-maintained counters.
434	- `applications.listing_version_id` — makes cascade a single UPDATE.
435	- `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` — generated columns (MAX across pre-conditions per platform), btree-indexed for feed filter.
436	
437	### 4.4 RLS Model
438	
439	Custom JWT issued by our `/auth/*` edge functions (signed with project JWT secret). Payload:
440	```json
441	{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
442	```
443	RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`. JWT is signed by us, not user-editable. Safe for authz.
444	
445	### 4.5 Indexes
446	
447	- Feed: partial index `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
448	- Eligibility pre-filter: generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` btree-indexed.
449	- Application uniqueness: partial unique `(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status in ('pending','approved')`.
450	- Notifications: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.
451	
452	### 4.6 Triggers / Functions
453	
454	All in `app_private` schema (security definer).
455	
456	| Function | Trigger |
457	|---|---|
458	| `bump_listing_version()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if versioned field changed, INSERT new version, cascade-cancel pending apps, emit notifications. |
459	| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — updates `creator_profiles`. |
460	| `submission_side_effects()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — increments count. (Auto-close-on-max deferred to v1.1.) |
461	| `emit_event()` | Helper called by state-changing functions. |

1103	-- Core bump routine — called directly (from listings UPDATE trigger) or via the
1104	-- edge function PATCH /listings/:id after it has applied listing_conditions/sample_videos edits.
1105	-- The edge function is the sole writer to listing_conditions + sample_videos once a listing is active;
1106	-- it calls app_private.request_listing_version_bump(listing_id, reason, changed_fields) AFTER
1107	-- those child edits commit, which performs an UPDATE listings SET ... that fires this trigger.
1108	create or replace function app_private.bump_listing_version()
1109	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1110	declare
1111	  versioned_changed boolean;
1112	  new_version_id    uuid;
1113	  prev_version_id   uuid;
1114	  changed_fields    text[] := array[]::text[];
1115	begin
1116	  if old.price_cents     is distinct from new.price_cents     then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'price_cents'; end if;
1117	  if old.currency        is distinct from new.currency        then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'currency'; end if;
1118	  if old.max_submissions is distinct from new.max_submissions then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'max_submissions'; end if;
1119	  -- The edge function passes a cue via the dummy `version_bump_reason` text column (set-and-reset in same UPDATE)
1120	  -- to indicate listing_conditions or sample_videos were edited:
1121	  if new.version_bump_reason is not null then
1122	    changed_fields := changed_fields || new.version_bump_reason;  -- e.g. 'conditions' | 'sample_videos'
1123	    new.version_bump_reason := null;  -- consume the cue
1124	  end if;
1125	
1126	  versioned_changed := array_length(changed_fields, 1) > 0;
1127	  if versioned_changed and new.status = 'active' then
1128	    prev_version_id      := old.current_version_id;
1129	    new.version_number   := old.version_number + 1;
1130	    insert into public.listing_versions(listing_id, version_number, price_cents, currency, max_submissions, snapshot, previous_version_id, changed_fields)
1131	      values (new.id, new.version_number, new.price_cents, new.currency, new.max_submissions,
1132	              to_jsonb(new), prev_version_id, changed_fields)
1133	      returning id into new_version_id;
1134	    new.current_version_id := new_version_id;
1135	
1136	    -- Capture affected creators BEFORE we update their status (so we can notify only them).
1137	    with cascaded as (
1138	      update public.applications
1139	         set status = 'cancelled_listing_edit', updated_at = now()
1140	       where listing_id = new.id and status = 'pending'
1141	       returning creator_id
1142	    )
1143	    insert into public.notifications(user_id, kind, payload)
1144	      select c.creator_id, 'listing_version_changed',
1145	             jsonb_build_object(
1146	               'listing_id',          new.id,
1147	               'new_version',         new.version_number,
1148	               'previous_version_id', prev_version_id,
1149	               'new_version_id',      new_version_id,
1150	               'changed_fields',      to_jsonb(changed_fields)
1151	             )
1152	        from cascaded c;
1153	  end if;
1154	  return new;
1155	end $$;

1161	-- Helper the edge function calls after committing listing_conditions/sample_videos edits.
1162	-- Produces an UPDATE that fires trg_bump_listing_version with the appropriate cue.
1163	--
1164	-- ORDERING CONTRACT (must be followed by the edge function):
1165	--   The edge function owns the full edit txn. Inside ONE transaction it must:
1166	--     1. `select id from public.listings where id = :lid for update;` — serialize against POST /applications and concurrent edits.
1167	--     2. `insert into public.listing_versions (...) returning id into v_new;`
1168	--        (new version row with empty conditions/sample_videos initially)
1169	--     3. `insert into public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id=v_new, ...)` for each pre/post-condition.
1170	--     4. `insert into public.sample_videos (listing_version_id=v_new, ...)` for each sample.
1171	--     5. `select app_private.request_listing_version_bump(:lid, :reason);`
1172	--        This swaps `listings.current_version_id` to v_new atomically. The
1173	--        `trg_bump_listing_version` trigger then runs `refresh_listing_thresholds`
1174	--        against the now-populated v_new, cascades applications, and emits
1175	--        notifications. Because conditions were inserted in step 3 against v_new
1176	--        — which is NOT yet `current_version_id` until step 5 — the per-row
1177	--        `refresh_listing_thresholds()` trigger on `listing_conditions` becomes
1178	--        a no-op (it filters `where listings.current_version_id = NEW.listing_version_id`),
1179	--        so thresholds are computed exactly once, against the full set, at step 5.
1180	--     6. commit.
1181	--
1182	--   Feed queries never observe a partial-conditions state: conditions are
1183	--   written to a version that is not current, then made current atomically.
1184	--
1185	--   The `refresh_listing_thresholds()` trigger on listing_conditions is therefore
1186	--   MUST-filter by `current_version_id = NEW.listing_version_id` and return
1187	--   early otherwise. (See §15 trigger definition — keep this invariant.)
1188	create or replace function app_private.request_listing_version_bump(
1189	  p_listing_id uuid, p_reason text
1190	) returns uuid
1191	  language plpgsql security definer as $$
1192	declare new_ver uuid;
1193	begin
1194	  if p_reason not in ('conditions', 'sample_videos') then
1195	    raise exception 'invalid version bump reason %', p_reason;
1196	  end if;
1197	  update public.listings
1198	     set version_bump_reason = p_reason,
1199	         updated_at = now()

1200	   where id = p_listing_id
1201	  returning current_version_id into new_ver;
1202	  return new_ver;
1203	end $$;

1226	-- Race-free latest-row promotion + denormalization.
1227	-- Fired BEFORE INSERT so we hold a row-level lock on social_links via advisory lock
1228	-- for the (social_link, scrape_mode) pair. This ensures concurrent webhook redeliveries
1229	-- and manual refreshes cannot both set is_latest=true.
1230	create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
1231	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1232	declare
1233	  sl     public.social_links%rowtype;
1234	  lock_key bigint;
1235	begin
1236	  if new.status <> 'fresh' then
1237	    -- 'refreshing' and 'failed' rows never become latest, never denormalize.
1238	    new.is_latest := false;
1239	    return new;
1240	  end if;
1241	
1242	  -- Serialize per (social_link_id, scrape_mode). hashtext gives a stable bigint mapping.
1243	  lock_key := hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
1244	  perform pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);
1245	
1246	  -- Demote any previous latest for this (social_link, scrape_mode) pair.
1247	  update public.metric_snapshots
1248	     set is_latest = false
1249	   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
1250	     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
1251	     and is_latest;
1252	
1253	  -- Claim latest for this new row.
1254	  new.is_latest := true;
1255	
1256	  -- Denormalize into creator_profiles — only columns owned by this scrape_mode.
1257	  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
1258	
1259	  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile' then
1260	    update public.creator_profiles
1261	       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
1262	           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
1263	           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
1264	           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
1265	           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
1266	           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
1267	           updated_at               = now()
1268	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
1269	
1270	  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details' then
1271	    update public.creator_profiles
1272	       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
1273	           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
1274	           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
1275	           updated_at               = now()
1276	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
1277	
1278	  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts' then
1279	    update public.creator_profiles
1280	       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
1281	           metrics_fetched_at          = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
1282	           updated_at                  = now()
1283	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
1284	  end if;
1285	
1286	  return new;
1287	end $$;

1312	-- Keeps listings.active_pending_applications_count in sync. Drives design §2.5
1313	-- age-bucketed nudge banners and the lister Dashboard "N waiting" tile. The
1314	-- counter reflects applications currently in status='pending' only.
1315	--
1316	-- Tracked transitions (new → effect):
1317	--   INSERT pending                          → +1
1318	--   UPDATE pending → anything else          → -1
1319	--   UPDATE anything else → pending          → +1   (rare: withdraw-undo not in v1, but safe)
1320	--   DELETE where old.status='pending'       → -1
1321	create or replace function app_private.maintain_pending_apps_counter()
1322	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1323	declare
1324	  delta smallint := 0;
1325	  target uuid;
1326	begin
1327	  if tg_op = 'INSERT' then
1328	    if new.status = 'pending' then delta := 1; target := new.listing_id; end if;
1329	  elsif tg_op = 'DELETE' then
1330	    if old.status = 'pending' then delta := -1; target := old.listing_id; end if;
1331	  elsif tg_op = 'UPDATE' then
1332	    if new.status = 'pending' and old.status <> 'pending' then delta := 1;  target := new.listing_id; end if;
1333	    if old.status = 'pending' and new.status <> 'pending' then delta := -1; target := new.listing_id; end if;
1334	    -- listing_id is immutable on applications, so old.listing_id = new.listing_id.
1335	  end if;
1336	
1337	  if delta <> 0 then
1338	    update public.listings
1339	       set active_pending_applications_count = active_pending_applications_count + delta
1340	     where id = target;
1341	  end if;
1342	  return coalesce(new, old);
1343	end $$;

1350	-- 15b. Listing threshold maintenance
1351	-- =========================================================
1352	-- Keeps listings.min_followers_tiktok/instagram in sync with the MAX pre-condition
1353	-- threshold across the listing's CURRENT version. Fired on any write to listing_conditions.
1354	create or replace function app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds()
1355	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1356	declare
1357	  v_listing_id       uuid;
1358	  v_current          uuid;
1359	  v_affected_version uuid;
1360	begin
1361	  v_affected_version := coalesce(new.listing_version_id, old.listing_version_id);
1362	  -- Resolve the listing_id from the condition's version (works for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE).
1363	  select l.id, l.current_version_id
1364	    into v_listing_id, v_current
1365	    from public.listings l
1366	    join public.listing_versions lv on lv.listing_id = l.id
1367	   where lv.id = v_affected_version;
1368	
1369	  if v_listing_id is null then
1370	    return coalesce(new, old);
1371	  end if;
1372	
1373	  -- Ordering-contract invariant: edge function inserts conditions against a
1374	  -- version that is NOT yet current; only request_listing_version_bump swaps
1375	  -- current_version_id. If the affected row belongs to a non-current version,
1376	  -- skip — thresholds will be recomputed by refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump
1377	  -- when the version swap happens, and at that point the full condition set is
1378	  -- already in place. This prevents partial-set reads during multi-condition edits.
1379	  if v_affected_version <> v_current then
1380	    return coalesce(new, old);
1381	  end if;
1382	
1383	  update public.listings
1384	     set min_followers_tiktok = (
1385	           select max(numeric_threshold)::integer
1386	             from public.listing_conditions
1387	            where listing_version_id = v_current
1388	              and kind     = 'pre'
1389	              and metric   = 'min_followers'
1390	              and platform = 'tiktok'
1391	         ),
1392	         min_followers_instagram = (
1393	           select max(numeric_threshold)::integer
1394	             from public.listing_conditions
1395	            where listing_version_id = v_current
1396	              and kind     = 'pre'
1397	              and metric   = 'min_followers'
1398	              and platform = 'instagram'
1399	         ),
1400	         updated_at = now()
1401	   where id = v_listing_id;
1402	
1403	  return coalesce(new, old);
1404	end $$;

1410	-- When a listing's current_version_id changes (version bump), recompute thresholds
1411	-- against the new version's condition set.
1412	create or replace function app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump()
1413	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1414	begin
1415	  if new.current_version_id is distinct from old.current_version_id then
1416	    update public.listings
1417	       set min_followers_tiktok = (
1418	             select max(numeric_threshold)::integer
1419	               from public.listing_conditions
1420	              where listing_version_id = new.current_version_id
1421	                and kind = 'pre' and metric = 'min_followers' and platform = 'tiktok'
1422	           ),
1423	           min_followers_instagram = (
1424	             select max(numeric_threshold)::integer
1425	               from public.listing_conditions
1426	              where listing_version_id = new.current_version_id
1427	                and kind = 'pre' and metric = 'min_followers' and platform = 'instagram'
1428	           )
1429	     where id = new.id;
1430	  end if;
1431	  return new;
1432	end $$;
1433	
1434	create trigger trg_listings_refresh_thresholds_on_bump
1435	  after update of current_version_id on public.listings
1436	  for each row execute function app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump();
1437	
1438	-- =========================================================
1439	-- 15c. Session revocation (JWT denylist)
1440	-- =========================================================
1441	create table public.revoked_sessions (
1442	  session_id uuid primary key,
1443	  user_id    uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
1444	  revoked_at timestamptz not null default now(),
1445	  reason     text,
1446	  -- Set to the session's original `exp` claim so cleanup can safely prune
1447	  -- after the JWT is no longer presentable anyway.
1448	  expires_at timestamptz not null
1449	);
1450	create index on public.revoked_sessions (user_id);
1451	create index on public.revoked_sessions (expires_at);
1452	
1453	-- Cache semantics (each edge-function container instance):
1454	--   * In-memory Set<session_id> keyed by session_id, loaded from
1455	--     `select session_id from public.revoked_sessions where expires_at > now()`.
1456	--   * TTL: 5 minutes. (Was 15. Tightened because custom-auth revocation latency
1457	--     is security-material — 5 min is the upper bound on how long a stolen token
1458	--     can still be used after the user clicks "log out everywhere".)
1459	--   * LISTEN channel `revoked_sessions_invalidate` — a NOTIFY is emitted by a
1460	--     trigger on INSERT carrying the new session_id as payload; the container
1461	--     adds it to the Set immediately without a DB roundtrip.
1462	--   * Reconnect policy: whenever the LISTEN connection drops for ANY reason
1463	--     (deploy, network blip, Postgres restart, idle timeout), on successful
1464	--     reconnect the container MUST execute a full reload query — otherwise
1465	--     revocations that happened during the disconnect are lost until the next
1466	--     TTL expiry. `listen_reconnected` events are logged for ops visibility.
1467	--   * Cold start: load the full set synchronously before the container accepts
1468	--     its first request. A cold container accepting requests with a stale
1469	--     denylist is the worst case we're avoiding.
1470	--   * Query path: `if (denylist.has(jwt.session_id)) reject 401`. O(1).
1471	--
1472	-- A JWT presenting a `session_id` that is missing from the denylist but present
1473	-- in the table (e.g., TTL just expired and NOTIFY hasn't fired yet) falls back
1474	-- to a point-lookup on Postgres for `session_id IN :set-of-sids-from-this-request`.
1475	-- This covers the 5-min window without flooding the DB for every request.
1476	create or replace function app_private.notify_revoked_session()
1477	returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
1478	begin
1479	  perform pg_notify('revoked_sessions_invalidate', new.session_id::text);
1480	  return new;
1481	end $$;
1482	create trigger trg_revoked_sessions_notify
1483	  after insert on public.revoked_sessions
1484	  for each row execute function app_private.notify_revoked_session();
1485	
1486	-- =========================================================
1487	-- 15d. Signup abuse throttle (per-IP)
1488	-- =========================================================
1489	-- Rolling window of signup attempts keyed by IP hash. Edge function rejects the 6th
1490	-- attempt from the same IP within 1 hour. Cleaned up by cron.
1491	create table public.signup_attempts (
1492	  id            uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
1493	  ip_hash       text not null,                 -- sha256 of client IP + salt
1494	  user_agent    text,
1495	  role_attempted user_role not null,
1496	  success       boolean not null,
1497	  created_at    timestamptz not null default now()
1498	);
1499	create index on public.signup_attempts (ip_hash, created_at desc);
1500	
1501	-- =========================================================
1502	-- 16. Cron
1503	-- =========================================================
1504	-- Mark snapshots stale after 24h (surfaces the "Outdated" badge in UI)
1505	select cron.schedule('mark-metrics-stale', '0 * * * *', $$
1506	  update public.metric_snapshots
1507	     set status = 'stale'
1508	   where status = 'fresh' and fetched_at < now() - interval '24 hours' and is_latest;
1509	$$);
1510	
1511	-- Janitor for stuck `refreshing` snapshots: if the webhook was lost (payload signed
1512	-- with a rotated secret, endpoint outage during delivery, etc.) the snapshot sits
1513	-- `refreshing` forever. After 10 min we fail-close so the creator sees an error
1514	-- and manual refresh becomes available again.
1515	select cron.schedule('fail-stuck-refreshing', '*/5 * * * *', $$
1516	  update public.metric_snapshots
1517	     set status = 'failed',
1518	         error_message = coalesce(error_message, 'webhook_lost')
1519	   where status = 'refreshing' and fetched_at < now() - interval '10 minutes';
1520	$$);
1521	
1522	-- Notifications retention: drop read notifications older than 90 days.
1523	select cron.schedule('prune-read-notifications', '0 3 * * *', $$
1524	  delete from public.notifications
1525	   where read_at is not null and read_at < now() - interval '90 days';
1526	$$);
1527	
1528	-- Signup attempt log retention: drop rows > 7d.
1529	select cron.schedule('prune-signup-attempts', '0 3 * * *', $$
1530	  delete from public.signup_attempts where created_at < now() - interval '7 days';
1531	$$);
1532	
1533	-- Revoked-sessions retention: drop rows past their original JWT expiry (safe to forget).
1534	select cron.schedule('prune-expired-revocations', '0 3 * * *', $$
1535	  delete from public.revoked_sessions where expires_at < now() - interval '1 day';
1536	$$);
1537	
1538	-- Application/submission expiration crons: INTENTIONALLY NOT SCHEDULED — no auto-expire in v1.
1539	```

1543	## 5. API / Edge Function Spec Sheet
1544	
1545	**Transport mix:**
1546	- **Direct PostgREST** for RLS-safe reads (feed browse, notifications inbox).
1547	- **Edge Functions** for (a) external Apify calls, (b) multi-table atomic writes, (c) business logic, (d) JWT issuance, (e) webhook receivers.
1548	- **Realtime channels** on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`.
1549	
1550	Role = custom-JWT `role` claim.
1551	
1552	### 5.1 Auth
1553	
1554	**`POST /auth/signup-creator`**
1555	```ts
1556	interface Req {
1557	  username: string;
1558	  tiktok_handle?: string;
1559	  instagram_handle?: string;        // at least one of the two
1560	  display_name?: string;
1561	  country?: string;
1562	}
1563	interface Res {
1564	  user_id: string;
1565	  jwt: string;
1566	  social_links: { id: string; platform: Platform; handle: string; status: 'linked' }[];
1567	  initial_scrape_queued: boolean;
1568	}
1569	```
1570	**Transaction boundary:** the identity rows (`users`, `creator_profiles`, `social_links`, and pre-created `refreshing` `metric_snapshots` rows) commit in a single Postgres transaction. Apify runs are dispatched **after** the transaction commits — a dispatch failure does NOT roll back the account. The creator gets a working account, the `social_links` row, and a `refreshing` snapshot; the UI displays "Couldn't fetch metrics — we'll retry automatically" and the daily cron picks the link up within 24h. This is intentional: Apify outages shouldn't block signup.
1571	
1572	**Per-run outcomes after the commit** (handled inline in the edge function before returning):
1573	- If `.call(input, { waitSecs: 60 })` returns with `status: 'SUCCEEDED'` and items → parse + UPDATE snapshot to `fresh` inline. Counts toward the response's `initial_scrape_queued = false` flag per-platform.
1574	- If it returns with `status: 'RUNNING'` (inside-wait timeout) → webhook completes later. `initial_scrape_queued = true`.
1575	- If it throws (rate limit, 4xx/5xx from Apify) → UPDATE snapshot to `failed` with `error_message`, increment `social_links.fail_count`, return normally. The UI chips the failed mode as "Couldn't fetch" with a retry button.
1576	
1577	Signup is idempotent on `(username)` — a duplicate call returns `409 USERNAME_TAKEN` before any Apify dispatch.
1578	
1579	**Per-IP throttle:** the edge function computes `sha256(ip + SIGNUP_SALT)` and inserts a `signup_attempts` row. If ≥5 attempts from the same `ip_hash` occurred in the prior 60 minutes (regardless of success/failure), return `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS` with `retry_after_sec`. Salt rotates quarterly.
1580	
1581	Errors: `400 MISSING_HANDLE`, `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`, `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS`.
1582	
1583	**`POST /auth/signup-lister`**
1584	```ts
1585	interface Req { username: string; email: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string; }
1586	interface Res { user_id: string; jwt: string; }
1587	```
1588	Same per-IP throttle as `signup-creator` (shared `signup_attempts` table).
1589	Errors: `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 EMAIL_TAKEN`, `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS`.
1590	
1591	**`POST /auth/signout`**
1592	```ts
1593	interface Req { session_id: string; }
1594	interface Res { ok: true; }
1595	```
1596	Inserts a row into `revoked_sessions` with `expires_at = <jwt exp claim>`. Edge middleware caches the revocation set in-memory (5-min TTL; `LISTEN revoked_sessions_invalidate` pushes inserts live, with force-reload on reconnect — see §15c comments). JWTs with `session_id` in the set are rejected with `401`. Max stale-revocation window = 5 min (TTL) worst-case after a container cold-start that misses a NOTIFY during the one-request reload gap; typical window is zero because NOTIFY is synchronous.
1597

1598	### 5.2 Social Handles & Metrics
1599	
1600	**`POST /social/add-handle`** — creator links an additional platform
1601	```ts
1602	interface Req { platform: Platform; handle: string; }
1603	interface Res { social_link_id: string; scrape_run_id: string; }
1604	```
1605	Side effects: insert `social_links` + kick initial Apify run (async, webhook-completed).
1606	Errors: `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`, `409 PLATFORM_ALREADY_LINKED`.
1607	
1608	**`POST /social/update-handle`** — creator changed their username on the platform
1609	```ts
1610	interface Req { social_link_id: string; new_handle: string; }
1611	interface Res { ok: true; scrape_run_id: string; }
1612	```
1613	Side effects: update handle + trigger fresh scrape. Old snapshots retained.
1614	Errors: `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`.
1615	
1616	**`POST /social/unlink`**
1617	```ts
1618	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
1619	interface Res { ok: true; }
1620	```
1621	Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` (creator must keep ≥1 linked platform).
1622	
1623	**`POST /metrics/refresh`** — creator-triggered, rate-limited
1624	```ts
1625	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
1626	interface Res {
1627	  snapshot_ids: string[];  // 1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram (details + posts)
1628	  status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh';
1629	  retry_after_sec?: number;
1630	}
1631	```
1632	Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.
1633	
1634	| Platform | scrape_modes triggered | Actor runs |
1635	|---|---|---|
1636	| `tiktok` | `tiktok_profile` | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` × 1 |
1637	| `instagram` | `ig_details`, `ig_posts` | `apify/instagram-scraper` × 2 (different `resultsType`) |
1638	
1639	Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (with `retry_after_sec`), `502 APIFY_ERROR`.
1640	
1641	**Internal cron-invoked function `cron-refresh-metrics`**
1642	- Runs daily (scheduled via Supabase `pg_cron` → edge function).
1643	- Picks up to `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET` links with `metrics_fetched_at < now() - 24h AND status != 'failed_fetch'`.
1644	- Fires the appropriate scrape_modes per link (TikTok=1 run, IG=2 runs). All async with our webhook.
1645	- **Overlap guard.** Apify slowdowns or budget pressure can cause a run to take longer than 24h, so the next cron firing could overlap its predecessor. The edge function opens its DB transaction with:
1646	  ```sql
1647	  -- 0xC17DEF1E15 is an arbitrary 64-bit key reserved for this cron. Returns
1648	  -- false immediately if another session holds it — the function then exits
1649	  -- with a `cron_refresh_skipped_overlap` event (for ops visibility) and no
1650	  -- Apify runs are queued.
1651	  select pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtextextended('cron-refresh-metrics', 0));
1652	  ```
1653	  The lock auto-releases at txn end (session-scoped via `pg_advisory_lock` would leak on container crash; `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` is safer). This guarantees at-most-one active refresh cron, regardless of how far behind the previous one ran. The 24h cadence combined with the 10-min `fail-stuck-refreshing` janitor means no single slow run can stall the pipeline indefinitely.
1654	
1655	### 5.3 Apify Webhook Receiver
1656	
1657	**`POST /webhooks/apify`** — called by Apify when a run finishes
1658	```ts
1659	interface Req {  // Apify payload + our payloadTemplate vars
1660	  eventType:
1661	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'
1662	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED'
1663	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT'
1664	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED';
1665	  resource: { id: string; defaultDatasetId: string; status: string; actId: string; };
1666	  // injected via payloadTemplate when we started the run:
1667	  social_link_id: string;
1668	  scrape_mode: 'tiktok_profile' | 'ig_details' | 'ig_posts';
1669	  run_id: string;
1670	  status: string;
1671	  dataset_id: string;
1672	}
1673	interface Res { ok: true; }
1674	```
1675	Security: verify `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header (constant-time compare) against `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Idempotent by `run_id` (partial unique index on `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id`).
1676	
1677	Flow:
1678	1. Look up the pre-created `metric_snapshots` row by `apify_run_id`. If missing (rare edge case — initial insert failed), create one in `refreshing` state so later steps are idempotent.
1679	2. If `eventType !== 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'`: prepare `status_next=failed`, fill `error_message`, short-circuit to step 5.
1680	3. Fetch dataset items via `client.dataset(dataset_id).listItems({ clean: true })`.
1681	4. Parse per-scrape_mode (see §3e table) — set `follower_count`/`avg_views_last_10`/etc. columns the mode owns, leave others null.
1682	5. **Guarded terminal-state UPDATE** — crucial for Apify's retry semantics, which can deliver `SUCCEEDED` first and a retried `ABORTED` for the same `run_id` later (or vice versa). The update only transitions OUT OF `refreshing`, never between terminal states:
1683	   ```sql
1684	   update public.metric_snapshots
1685	      set status        = :status_next,         -- 'fresh' | 'failed'
1686	          raw_payload   = :items,
1687	          error_message = :error_message,
1688	          -- fetched_at stamped at run-completion, not at webhook-arrival,
1689	          -- so retries don't shift the timestamp forward.
1690	          fetched_at    = :apify_finished_at
1691	    where apify_run_id = :run_id
1692	      and status       = 'refreshing';          -- guard: do nothing if already terminal
1693	   ```
1694	   If zero rows are affected, log a `webhook_duplicate` event and return `200 OK` — idempotent no-op. The first terminal signal wins; later retries are ignored. This keeps a fresh snapshot from being clobbered by a retried-ABORTED.
1695	6. Trigger fires `denorm_metrics()` → updates only the `creator_profiles` columns owned by this `scrape_mode`. (Note: `denorm_metrics()` already short-circuits when the new row's status is not `fresh` — see §15 trigger — so the no-op guard in step 5 also keeps the denorm path clean.)
1696	7. On failure: increment `social_links.fail_count`; if ≥3 consecutive failures across all scrape_modes → `social_links.status=failed_fetch` + notify creator. On success: reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects *uninterrupted* failures.
1697	
1698	**IG consistency note:** Because IG needs two runs per refresh, `creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` will bump *twice* per refresh (once per webhook). The UI treats "fresh" as `min(metrics_fetched_at_across_modes)`. If one run fails and the other succeeds, the creator still sees a partial update — the failed mode's old value remains stale-but-shown, with a subtle warning chip. (Detail reconciled via `metric_snapshots.status` per mode.)

1747	### 5.6 Applications
1748	
1749	**`POST /applications`**
1750	```ts
1751	interface Req { listing_id: string; cover_note?: string; }
1752	interface Res { application_id: string; listing_version_id: string; }
1753	```
1754	Server re-evaluates eligibility; notifies lister.
1755	Errors: `403 INELIGIBLE`, `409 ALREADY_APPLIED`, `409 LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE`, `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED`.
1756	
1757	**Concurrency contract — closes the race with `bump_listing_version`.** Without row locks, an applicant's `INSERT applications` can interleave with a lister's `UPDATE listings` that triggers the version bump + cascade-cancel, leaving a fresh `pending` row pinned to an already-stale version. The insert txn must serialize on the listing row:
1758	
1759	```sql
1760	begin;
1761	-- 1. Pin the listing to this txn. Both POST /applications and the edge-function
1762	--    wrapper of bump_listing_version must take this lock. The bump trigger runs
1763	--    under the UPDATE's own row lock, so they are mutually exclusive.
1764	select id, status, current_version_id
1765	  from public.listings
1766	 where id = :listing_id

1826	**RLS enforcement on Realtime:** Supabase Realtime runs every broadcast row through the table's SELECT policy before sending, so a creator who subscribes to `applications:creator_id=<otherId>` receives zero rows — the underlying policy `creator_id = auth.jwt()->>'sub'::uuid` filters rows at the database, not client-side. 2004	## 8. MVP Scope Cuts

2006	Everything below is **explicitly out of v1** and tracked for v1.1 / v2 consideration. None of this is an oversight; each item was considered and cut.

2009	2. **No OAuth / platform integrations.** Apify does all scraping.
2010	3. **No IG Business/Creator requirement.** Apify works on personal accounts too.
2011	4. **No post-submission performance tracking.** v2 "Marketify Analytics".
2012	5. **No lister dashboard analytics.** Counts only.
2013	6. **No admin moderation console.** Reports land in a table; admins work via Supabase Studio.
2014	7. **No revision-request flow.** Binary approve/reject only.
2015	8. **No push notifications.** In-app + Realtime only.
2016	9. **No auto-expire** for applications, submissions, or listings — manual close only.

2019	12. **No blocklists.** Report-only.

2021	14. **No dark mode.** v2.
